title: NY Fed Repo Operations
summary: The attempt to fetch NY Fed repo operation data failed with a 400 Bad Request error. No actual repo operation data was retrieved. This represents a failed data fetch rather than a source document containing market information.
key_points: API request to NY Fed repo operations endpoint returned 400 Bad Request error
No actual repo operation data (terms, amounts, counterparties) was retrieved
Target topic was market structure / repo operations but source contains only error message
The underlying market plumbing data remains unrecorded
why_it_matters: Without actual repo operation data, market participants cannot assess current NY Fed lending operations, which are key tools for managing short-term interest rates and bank liquidity.
market_structure_context: The intended source would have provided repo operation details relevant to Fed market plumbing, but the API fetch failed, yielding no data on term lengths, offered amounts, or participant usage.

issues_found: Source contains only a 400 API error message with no actual market structure data
No extractable facts about NY Fed repo operations (terms, amounts, counterparties, rates)
Record provides no market-relevant information; only documents a failed data retrieval attempt
The 'market_notice' event type implies actual operational data was posted, but source has none
Archival value is zero—no content to preserve for market structure research

human_review.notes: Source is a failed API response with no actual content. While the record accurately describes the error, there is nothing to archive. The record should be rejected and the data fetch should be retried with corrected API parameters before creating a new record.

notes: No content. Source body is a 400 Bad Request error message: 'Failed to fetch NY Fed repo operation data: 400 Client Error: Bad Request for url: https://markets.newyorkfed.org/api/repo/all/results/latest.json'. The API endpoint likely requires different parameters or the endpoint path has changed. Recommend manual retrieval of current repo operation results from NY Fed Markets website or API documentation review.
